commit.message:
Add context tokens field to Local mode

Adds a persistent "Context tokens" field in the Local Settings box
(below the Model field, default 32768). At launch, the value is
injected as OLLAMA_NUM_CTX and LM_STUDIO_NUM_CTX so both Ollama and
LM Studio use the configured context window. Values below 32768 are
clamped. The setting is saved and restored across sessions.

commit.message:
ping_agent, auth_agent: increase batch size from 500 to 2000

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(model-3d): rebuild HEMesh after push-pull so face splitting works

After push-pull creates/modifies geometry via Manifold, the HEMesh
becomes stale. Now we rebuild it from the new BufferGeometry so that
line-tool face splitting works on push-pull-created boxes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(model-3d): build HEMesh directly at box creation, avoid lossy conversion

The round-trip BoxGeometry → HEMesh → BufferGeometry was corrupting
geometry. Now boxes are created with HEMesh as the source of truth,
and LineTool only attempts face splitting on objects that already
have an HEMesh (no more ensureHEMesh conversion).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(model-3d): use point-in-face lookup instead of stale triangle index

ensureHEMesh() rebuilds the geometry from the HEMesh, which changes all
triangle indices. The targetFaceIndex captured during pickDrawingPlane
was stale by the time finalizeLine used it.

Fix: add findFaceAtPoint() which does a 3D point-on-plane check + 2D
point-in-polygon test to find the correct HE face from the line's start
point, independent of triangle indices.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix crawler pipeline: remove broken partition filter and fix Flyway version

- Remove partition-based URL filtering from CrawlScheduler that silently
  dropped URLs dequeued by the wrong downloader instance
- Remove incompatible flyway-database-postgresql:10.8.1 dependency that
  crashed url-seen (AbstractMethodError with flyway-core 9.x)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

ping_agent: remove status updates, delegate to auth_agent

Ping agent now only records metrics and updates last_ping_at.
Status (online/offline) is managed exclusively by auth_agent.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(core): add RedisRateLimiter with sorted sets and Lua script (#201) (#206)

* feat(core): add RedisRateLimiter with sorted sets and Lua script (#201)

Implement a distributed sliding-window rate limiter backed by Redis
sorted sets. An atomic Lua script (ZREMRANGEBYSCORE + ZCARD + ZADD)
ensures no race conditions across replicas. Fail-open on Redis errors.
Supports configurable key prefix for multi-tenant isolation. Registers
with ShutdownCoordinator for clean connection close.

Also adds the PRD for the Redis rate limiter feature set.

23 unit tests with fakeredis[lua]; all 40 existing tests pass.

Fixes #201

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* chore(dev): add fakeredis[lua] to test dependencies

CI needs fakeredis with Lua scripting support to run
RedisRateLimiter unit tests.

fix: unify HAL device authority over audio pipeline

HAL device state changes (disable, remove, pin reconfigure) now properly
propagate to the legacy DAC/I2S audio path:

- hal_apply_config() DISABLE/RE-ENABLE for DAC-path devices uses the
  existing deferred toggle mechanism (_pendingDacToggle/_pendingEs8311Toggle)
  to trigger proper legacy teardown/init via main loop
- dac_output_deinit() preserves HAL_STATE_MANUAL set by HAL disable path
  instead of overwriting with HAL_STATE_UNAVAILABLE
- dac_output_init() delegation guard keeps existing HalDacAdapter on
  re-enable instead of removing and re-creating at different slot
- dac_output_init() re-enable path re-registers pipeline sink (cleared
  by deinit) so audio resumes after toggle cycle
- i2s_audio_enable_tx() reads HAL config for TX data pin instead of
  using hardcoded compile-time constant
- HAL API DELETE endpoint triggers DAC-path legacy teardown before
  device removal
- Same fixes applied to ES8311 secondary DAC path

commit.message:
fix(engine,types): simplify state management and remove aggregate hacks

- Updated SetCurrent/SetPlan/SetInternal to always use StoreState,
  letting it handle cycle-based batching internally.
- Removed manual AggregateChanges calls from tunnel initialization.
- This ensures Initialize() writes are immediately visible to agents
  without bypassing validation.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add cron/scheduling system with isolated sessions and delivery modes

Full scheduling system for background agent automation:
- Cron expressions (5-field standard syntax) with timezone support
- One-shot reminders that auto-disable after firing
- Isolated sessions per job (don't pollute main conversation)
- Delivery modes: silent (log only), conversation, Slack, Telegram
- Session reaper auto-cleans expired run logs (configurable retention)
- Lightweight built-in cron parser (no external dependencies)
- CRUD API routes with validation and audit logging
- UI on agent settings page for creating/managing schedules
- 30 tests for cron parsing, matching, next-time calculation, and scheduler

commit.message:
Fix referral leakage ROI always showing $600K

The leakage query had LIMIT 50, so len(leakage) was always 50 for
popular specialties, making the revenue estimate static. Now uses a
separate COUNT query to get the true number of leaking GPs for the
ROI calculation, while keeping LIMIT 50 for the display table only.

commit.message:
Track E: buildFseTable numBits bound — prove cells numBits ≤ accuracyLog (#795)

* refactor: deduplicate parseFrameHeader proofs and document grind usage

Deduplicate parseFrameHeader_pos_gt by deriving it from
parseFrameHeader_pos_ge_five (pos' ≥ pos + 5 → pos' > pos via omega),
eliminating ~25 lines of duplicated guard-peeling proof.

Document why grind is needed in decompressFrame_contentSize_eq and
decompressFrame_checksum_valid: split can't decompose match on
structure fields (header.contentSize, header.dictionaryId).

Closes #767

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* doc: add progress entry for Zstd.lean review recovery session

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: prove buildFseTable_numBits_le — cells numBits ≤ accuracyLog

Prove that every cell in the FSE decoding table constructed by
buildFseTable has numBits.toNat ≤ accuracyLog. This is condition (c)
of the ValidFseTable predicate, needed for proving FSE state
transitions stay in bounds during decoding.

The proof tracks a per-cell property through the four loops of
buildFseTable using forIn_range_preserves:
- Loops 1-2 set cells with default numBits = 0 (≤ al trivially)
- Loop 3 modifies only symbolCounts, not cells
- Loop 4 sets numBits := (al - log2 nextState).toUInt8, where
  (al - x) % 256 ≤ al by Nat.mod_le and Nat.sub_le

Introduces set!_preserves_forall helper for reasoning about
Array.set! preserving Fin-indexed predicates.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* doc: add progress entry for buildFseTable_numBits_le

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

commit.message:
docs(patterns): add mandatory SA mode propagation to all skill SKILL.md files (#365)

* docs(patterns): add mandatory SA mode propagation to all skill SKILL.md files

Add `--sa-mode true` to Quick Start examples and a standardized
"SA Mode Propagation (MANDATORY)" section across 17 SKILL.md files:
ai-reviewed-commit, code-review, codebase-audit, codebase-blog,
commit, csa-issue-reporter, csa-review, debate, dev-to-merge,
dev2merge, file-audit, mktd, mktsk, pr-codex-bot, review-loop,
sa, security-audit.

For the sa skill, all dispatch templates (planning, implementation,
trust verification) also updated with `--sa-mode true`.

This ensures prompt-guard propagation is maintained when CSA commands
are invoked from autonomous workflows (SA mode).

commit.message:
fix: upgrade to npm 11+ for OIDC trusted publisher support (#22)

OIDC trusted publishing requires npm >= 11.5.1. Node 22 ships with
npm 10.x which doesn't support this feature. Install npm@latest
globally, remove registry-url (injects GITHUB_TOKEN as auth), and
drop --provenance (automatic with trusted publishing).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add optimized v2 contract with gas improvements

Features:
- Custom errors instead of require strings (~1,000 gas saved)
- Unchecked blocks for safe operations (~200 gas saved)
- User token ID mapping for direct lookup
- Batch query functions for better frontend performance
- Contract stats function (single call for all data)
- Loop optimizations (~50 gas per iteration)

Total savings: ~2,400 gas per mint
Fully backward compatible with v1

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

commit.message:
refactor: remove Go from aqua, use native toolchain management

Go is a toolchain (compiler, linker, coverage tools), not a standalone
CLI. Managing it via aqua-proxy caused version mismatches when
-coverprofile subprocesses picked up a different Go binary from PATH.

- Remove golang/go from aqua.yaml (use Homebrew Go + go.mod toolchain)
- Remove GOTOOLCHAIN=local and grep workarounds from Taskfile.yml
- Document Go version management policy in development-tools.md

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add hero images to blog cards and fix keyword search

- Add thumbnail images (640w webp with jpg fallback) to all 11 blog cards
- Cards now use overflow-hidden with image at top, content in p-6 below
- Fix search: now searches visible card text (title + description) in
  addition to data-search keywords
- Fix search: split query into words and match each independently so
  "AI privacy" matches cards containing both words anywhere
- Clean up overly long data-search attributes on older cards

fix: switch to gemini-2.0-flash and handle LLM errors gracefully

gemini-2.5-flash free tier is limited to 20 RPD which is exhausted
when analyzing multiple companies (2 API calls per company).
gemini-2.0-flash has 1500 RPD free tier limit.

Also catch LLM exceptions per company so quota exhaustion skips
individual companies instead of crashing the entire workflow.
